rulings.Case Title: TROY SNOWDEN VS. S&S HOSPITALITY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Ccp 425.16 Special Motion To Strike Anti-Slapp Motion To First Amended Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2024, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT TROY SNOWDEN'S Special Motion To Strike Anti-Slapp To First Amended Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's Special Motion to Strike Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Plaintiff has not met the burden of identifying any claims arising out of protected activity. Because Plaintiff has not met its burden, the Court does not need to address whether any challenged claim is legally sufficient and factually substantiated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TROY SNOWDEN VS. S&S HOSPITALITY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2024, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT TROY SNOWDEN'S DEMURRER to Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant's Demurrer to Amended Cross-Complaint is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Cross-Complaint to allege facts, not conclusions, showing justifiable reliance by Cross-Complaint on Cross-Defendant's misrepresentations. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Paragraphs 2-8 of Chau Declaration are stricken. This is the second time is 3 months counsel submits, without any citation to authority "evidence" in conjunction with a pleading challenge. The Court further notes that Cross-Complainants oppositions have been filed late due to Cross-Complainants mistaken identification of the parties filing the papers. This is not the first time the timely submitted filings are rejected for mistakes of this kind (wrong party name; wrong party identification; wrong pleadings designation, etc). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TROY SNOWDEN VS. S&S HOSPITALITY INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2024, line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT TROY SNOWDEN'S MOTION TO STRIKE Amended CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-Defendant's Motion to Strike Amended Cross-Complaint is DENIED. Moving party failed to show that the First Amended Complaint, or any portions thereof, is improper, false or irrelevant or not drawn in conformity with the laws of the State of California. Paragraphs 2-8 of Chau Declaration are stricken. This is the second time is 3 months counsel submits, without any citation to authority "evidence" in conjunction with a pleading challenge. rulings.Case Title: JUSTIN MOORE ET AL VS. APARTMENT MANAGEMENT CONSULTANTS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2024, line 3. PLAINTIFF JUSTIN MOORE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Plaintiff's Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. Plaintiffs motion does not comply with CRC 3.1350(b). Triable issues of fact exists, including, 8, 9, 46, and 63. The Court notes that a motion for summary adjudication is framed by the pleadings. At the time the motion was filed and as late as 11:50 a.m. on December 16, 2024 there was no dismissal of Plaintiffs Epps and Maravilla-Guardado, the moving parties on this motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. JESSICA PERLA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiff?S Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant(S) And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $818 Against Defendant(S) Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 2033.280(C); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: On the Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 18, 2024, line 12. PLAINTIFF EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC'S Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiff's Requests For Admissions, Set One, To Defendant(s) And Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of $818 Against Defendant(S) Pursuant To California Code Of Civil Procedure 2033.280(C). Plaintiff's Motion to Deem Admissions Admitted is DENIED. Declaration is support of the motion is not dated. CCP 2015.5. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

PLAINTIFF EFREN VELONZA'S Motion For Order Deeming Admissions Admitted And For Sanctions Against Defendant Ernesto Velonza. Plaintiff's Motion to Deem Admissions Admitted is GRANTED, subject to opposition. Admissions are deemed admitted.
